Key: Following the breakout success of 'Purple Swag", Rocky began working on a new project titled "Mouth Fulla Gold" and had the full mixtape ready on May 16th, 2011. During that month, likely around the time “Wassup” was released, Rocky stopped selling drugs and started take his brothers advice and truly focus on his rapping career. Rocky would continue to work on "Mouth Fulla Gold" as it was reworked in June and until around July 5th, 2011 when the "Purple Swag" music video was released. On this day A$AP Yams took to his now deleted Tumblr "realniggatumblr" and mentioned the name "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" for the first time, which was then due at the end of July 2011.

Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: Instrumental for the song Toast To The Gods which is just "The Future" by Diddy which is produced by one half of Mobb Deep, Havoc.

(July 5th, 2011) (LIVE.LOVE.A$AP is mentioned for the first time by A$AP Yams)
(August 2011) (Mixtape was set to release, but is delayed for unknown reasons)
(October 3rd, 2011) (Mixtape was set to release on Rocky's birthday, but is delayed for unknown reasons)
(October 31st, 2011) (LIVE.LOVE.A$AP is released)
(November 29th, 2011) (Rocky announces a deluxe edition of LIVE.LOVE.A$AP)
(December 21st, 2011) (Rocky mentions the deluxe edition for the last time)
Key: Following the hype and high expectations from the first single's music video, Rocky then released another single on July 21st, 2011 titled “Peso”, another hit under Rocky’s belt. From here, the mixtape was then delayed to August for unknown reasons. Rocky later 'leaked' an early version of "Trilla" on September 17th, 2011. In early October, Rocky signed a revolutionary two-year $3 million dollar record deal. Finally after all the waiting, on Halloween 2011 the project “LIVE.LOVE.A$AP” was officially released. This mixtape is widely known for its slowed down, Houstan influenced, psychedelic and dream-like sounds which made it stand out from many other hip-hop projects from this time period and it would become a gigantic pioneer for the genre of cloud rap for many years to come. Much later in 2021 the mixtape was re-released on streaming services, missing certain songs such as "Kissin' Pink" "Out Of This World" and "Purple Swag: Chapter 2" along with featuring different versions of the songs "Leaf" and "Acid Drip", but added a new song titled “Sandman” dedicated to the late A$AP Yams with production by long time collaborator Clams Casino along with a newer producer Kelvin Krash.

Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: The instrumental for the song "Bass" which was released on Clams Casino's mixtape titled "Instrumentals 2" that was released on June 5th, 2013 after appearing on Rocky's "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" mixtape on October 31st, 2011. The song samples "Just for Now" by Imogen Heap which is a commonly used sample for the producer, Clams Casino, as he has used it on many songs before. The instrumental is in significantly higher quality than the released version of the song for unknown reasons, but an earlier version of the song with the higher quality version seemingly exists as it was previewed live on October 15th, 2011.

Brand New Guy
(feat. ScHoolboy Q) (prod. Don Cannon & Lyle Leduff)
Notes (Join the Rocky Hub Discord server: The instrumental for the Rocky & ScHoolboy Q song "Brand New Guy" produced by Don Cannon & Lyle Leduff. Seemingly posted online by Lyle Leduff himself and for now can only be found in badish quality with parts of it being distorted.

Instrumental for the song "Demons" which was released on Clams Casino's mixtape titled "Instrumentals" on March 7th, 2011 under the title "Numb" and was made before the release of "Demons". It has been said that Rocky found this instrumental and recorded over it before him and Clams Casino ever met and the song would appear on two mixtapes. One of these mixtapes having an unknown title that was deleted by an engineer with this song being one of the three recovered songs. The other mixtape is titled "Mouth Fulla Gold" where an early mix of the song would appear as track six. The song was later released on "LIVE.LOVE.A$AP" later that year on October 31st, 2011 under the final name of "Demons".
Instrumental for "Ghost X". Leaked on February 1st, 2025 as a bonus for the "Sometimes" groupbuy. Leaked as stereo files.
